summary: Technical decisions are first-class citizens of the repository — every decision affecting architecture, a contract, or behavior is written into the repo in a disposition a knowledge-graph indexer can extract.

rules.rule: Write every decision that affects architecture, a contract, or behavior into the repo in a disposition a knowledge-graph indexer can extract — a decision that lives only in a conversation is lost.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: A decision that must be indexable is written in Markdown/ADR — inline code markers are advisory-only for the human reader and never a substitute for the ADR; the inline comment links to the ADR.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: When work introduces or expands an architecture, contract, behavior standard, AI-driven behavior, production dependency, runtime/CDN/render source, or cross-tenant boundary, classify the decision before implementing and write the ADR/canon first — surfacing conversational triggers explicitly to the human authority rather than silently proceeding.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: Every recorded decision carries the explicit why and the rejected alternatives with reasons — the value to a future agent is knowing what not to do, and why.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: One decision has exactly one canonical place — architecture/must-be-indexable decisions live in an ADR or canon and the inline comment links to it; never duplicate the decision across places.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: Every retrospectively reconstructed decision carries the mandatory tag [RECONSTRUCTED from <source>], distinguishing direct evidence from retroactive inference — and reconstruction is opportunistic and high-value-first, never big-bang.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate
